title: Modeling of strength of high-performance concrete using artificial neural networks
main_contribution: Foundational ANN model for HPC compressive strength; origin of the canonical 1030-mix benchmark dataset
method_or_data: ANN on 1030 mixes with 8 mix-design inputs
limitations: Single property; no uncertainty/explainability; no link to data management or BIM
relevance_to_chapter: Primary modelling dataset and historical baseline for the strength model

title: Prediction of compressive strength of high-performance concrete based on multiple machine learning models
main_contribution: Compares individual vs ensemble learners on the 1030-sample UCI dataset
limitations: Stops at prediction accuracy; no element-level data management or OpenBIM
relevance_to_chapter: Confirms UCI dataset suitability and ensemble baselines

title: Machine learning and interactive GUI for concrete compressive strength prediction
main_contribution: Adds a usability layer (GUI) over strength prediction
method_or_data: Multiple ML regressors with deployed GUI
limitations: GUI is standalone; not interoperable with IFC/BIM or QA/QC workflows
relevance_to_chapter: Motivates moving beyond standalone tools toward standardised data carriers

title: Comparing the performance of machine learning models for predicting the compressive strength of concrete
main_contribution: Benchmarks ANN/tree models; ANN best R2 and RMSE
limitations: Prediction-only; no data-management or sustainability dimension
relevance_to_chapter: Supports model-selection rationale for the strength model

title: Machine learning in concrete durability: challenges and pathways identified by RILEM TC 315-DCS towards enhanced predictive models
main_contribution: Authoritative review (2013-2024) of ML for carbonation chloride sulfate frost shrinkage corrosion
limitations: Highlights data scarcity heterogeneity and poor generalisation of durability ML
relevance_to_chapter: Justifies treating durability as optional-with-fallback and need for data quality flags

title: Prediction and prevention of concrete chloride penetration: machine learning and MICP techniques
main_contribution: Ensemble models for chloride migration coefficient
method_or_data: Ensemble regressors on chloride test data
limitations: Property-specific; small heterogeneous data; no deployment carrier
relevance_to_chapter: Example durability target for the passport durability block

authors: Hosseinzadeh, M.; Mousavi, S.S.; Hosseinzadeh, A.; Dehestani, M.
title: An efficient machine learning approach for predicting concrete chloride resistance using a comprehensive dataset
main_contribution: ML for chloride resistance on a compiled dataset
method_or_data: ML regression on aggregated literature data
limitations: Heterogeneous sources; limited external validity
relevance_to_chapter: Documents durability-data availability and limitations

title: Explainable Machine Learning for Multicomponent Concrete: Predictive Modeling and Feature Interaction Insights
main_contribution: Uses SHAP to expose feature importance and interactions
limitations: Explanations rarely propagated into engineering data records
relevance_to_chapter: Basis for embedding SHAP top-features into each passport

title: Optimization and predictive performance of fly ash-based sustainable concrete using integrated multitask deep learning with interpretable ML
main_contribution: Multitask DL with SHAP+LIME for sustainable concrete
limitations: Explanations stay in notebooks; no standardised carrier
relevance_to_chapter: Supports multi-property + explainability framing

title: SCM-RAC multi-output machine learning framework for mechanical and durability properties of concrete
main_contribution: Predicts CS TS FS E slump drying shrinkage and chloride permeability jointly
method_or_data: Multi-output ML with selective information transfer; SCM/RAC mixes
limitations: Sparse reporting of some targets; research-code stage
relevance_to_chapter: Reference multi-property dataset/approach for durability extension

title: Insights into factors affecting the carbonation rate of concrete with SCMs revealed from data mining and machine learning
main_contribution: Data-mined carbonation dataset with up to 94% SCM binders
method_or_data: Data mining + ML on carbonation tests
limitations: Carbonation data still limited and heterogeneous
relevance_to_chapter: Supports SCM/durability discussion and data-scarcity argument

title: Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) 4.3 - IfcMaterial
main_contribution: Defines IfcMaterial as the core entity for material designation and properties
limitations: Spec defines structure not domain content like ML predictions
relevance_to_chapter: Anchor concept for mapping passport material fields

title: IFC 4.3 - Pset_ConcreteElementGeneral and concrete property sets
main_contribution: Standard property sets for concrete elements
limitations: No standard Pset captures ML prediction uncertainty carbon class or QA decision
relevance_to_chapter: Justifies a proposed custom Pset_ConcretePerformancePassport

title: A BIM-GIS-based approach for quality management aligned with ISO 9001 (QualiSite)
main_contribution: Links field inspection data to BIM elements; RC wall case study
limitations: Focus on inspection traceability not predictive/material performance
relevance_to_chapter: Shows BIM-QA systems stop at inspection records not ML/sustainability

title: Development and application of a digitalized construction quality document management system using BIM
venue: Journal of Construction Engineering and Management (ASCE)
main_contribution: BIM-linked quality document management framework
limitations: Document-centric; no predictive or sustainability integration
relevance_to_chapter: Evidence that BIM QC stays document-level

title: A review of construction management challenges and BIM-based solutions (schedule cost quality safety)
main_contribution: Reviews BIM-based QC and decision-making solutions
limitations: Quality strand rarely connects to material ML or EPD data
relevance_to_chapter: Positions BIM-QC scope and its gaps

title: Concept for a BIM-based Material Passport for buildings
venue: IOP Conf. Series: Earth and Environmental Science 225 012073
main_contribution: Foundational BIM-based material passport concept
limitations: Focus on materials inventory and recycling not performance/QA/durability
relevance_to_chapter: Core prior art the chapter extends from reuse toward performance

title: Deconstruction Information Model-based material passports for circular economy in AEC through BIM: a literature review
main_contribution: Reviews BIM-enabled MPs for end-of-life and reuse
limitations: MPs centred on EOL/deconstruction not in-service performance
relevance_to_chapter: Demonstrates passports usually stop at reuse/EOL

title: The material passport for a circular construction industry
main_contribution: Synthesises material-passport concepts and data needs
limitations: Circularity framing; performance/quality dimensions under-addressed
relevance_to_chapter: Confirms reuse-centric framing gap

title: A review of managing built asset information for material circularity
main_contribution: Reviews information management for material circularity
limitations: Information models target circularity not performance/sustainability QA
relevance_to_chapter: Supports data-management framing of passports

title: Enabling circularity in Turkish construction: a case of BIM-based material management utilizing material passports
main_contribution: Case study integrating MPs with BIM for material tracking
limitations: Tracking/reuse focus; no ML performance or carbon class
relevance_to_chapter: Reinforces reuse-centric scope of existing MPs

title: Analysis of limitations for the integration of EPD data in IFC
main_contribution: Identifies concrete limitations in representing EPD data within IFC
limitations: Highlights missing structures for environmental data in IFC
relevance_to_chapter: Directly motivates the OpenBIM carbon mapping and custom Pset

title: A review of data interoperability challenges and advanced methodologies from BIM to LCA
main_contribution: Reviews BIM-to-LCA interoperability barriers and methods
limitations: Interoperability still manual/spreadsheet-based in many cases
relevance_to_chapter: Frames data-management challenge the passport addresses

title: BIM and LCA integration: a systematic literature review
main_contribution: Systematic review of BIM-LCA workflows
limitations: Most workflows used spreadsheets; automation immature
relevance_to_chapter: Establishes long-standing BIM-LCA integration gap

title: Review of AI-assisted design of low-carbon cost-effective concrete toward carbon neutrality
main_contribution: Reviews ML property prediction + multi-objective low-carbon mix design
limitations: Optimisation-focused; outputs not packaged for BIM/QA data management
relevance_to_chapter: Positions AI-sustainability work the passport operationalises

title: Managing waste for production of low-carbon concrete mix using an uncertainty-aware machine learning model
main_contribution: Jointly predicts compressive strength and GWP with calibrated uncertainty
method_or_data: Explainable Boosting Regressor on 3114 mixes; jackknife+ intervals
limitations: Stops at prediction/optimisation; no OpenBIM carrier
relevance_to_chapter: Strong precedent for strength+GWP+uncertainty bundled together

title: Quantifying the impact of data quality on machine learning prediction: a large-scale study of construction product environmental declarations
venue: International Journal of Life Cycle Assessment (Springer)
main_contribution: Shows how EPD data quality affects ML reliability
limitations: Confirms EPD data heterogeneity/quality risks
relevance_to_chapter: Motivates data-quality and missing-data handling in passports

title: A construction-phase digital twin framework for quality assurance and decision support in civil infrastructure projects
main_contribution: Links inspection production early-age sensing and predictive strength to elements for readiness-based decisions
limitations: Preprint; not tied to OpenBIM IFC/IDS standards
relevance_to_chapter: Closest prior art for element-level QA/QC decisions; passport adds OpenBIM/IDS

title: C1074 Standard Practice for Estimating Concrete Strength by the Maturity Method
main_contribution: Standard for in-place strength estimation enabling formwork removal etc.
limitations: Requires temperature history; not predictive from mix alone
relevance_to_chapter: Grounds early-age/QA decision context

title: Extending BIM interoperability for real-time concrete formwork process monitoring
main_contribution: Couples embedded sensor data with BIM for formwork decisions
limitations: Sensor-specific; not a general performance data carrier
relevance_to_chapter: Shows BIM can carry construction-phase concrete data

title: Prediction of early age compressive strength of concrete using machine learning
main_contribution: IoT + ANN early-age strength prediction
limitations: Prediction-only; not packaged for QA records or BIM
relevance_to_chapter: Supports early-age strength as a passport input

title: Information Delivery Specification (IDS) v1.0 - final standard
main_contribution: Machine-readable specification of information requirements for IFC with automatic checking
limitations: Needs domain requirement definitions to be useful
relevance_to_chapter: Mechanism to validate Concrete Performance Passport requirements

title: Information Delivery Manual (IDM) - ISO 29481-1
main_contribution: Methodology to capture processes and information requirements
limitations: Process-level; pairs with IDS for checkable requirements
relevance_to_chapter: Frames information-requirement matrix for the passport

title: Embodied energy and carbon in construction materials (Inventory of Carbon and Energy ICE)
venue: Proceedings of the Institution of Civil Engineers - Energy 161(2) 87-98
main_contribution: Open embodied energy/carbon database for materials incl. cement/concrete
limitations: Cradle-to-gate generic factors; not mix-specific
relevance_to_chapter: Source of transparent per-constituent carbon factors

authors: Broyles, J.M.; Gevaudan, J.P.; Brown, N.C.
title: A compiled dataset of ready-mix concrete Environmental Product Declarations for life cycle assessment
venue: Data in Brief 52 109852 (dataset on Mendeley Data)
main_contribution: Large compiled dataset of U.S. ready-mix concrete EPDs with GWP and mix attributes
method_or_data: Compiled from ASTM/NRMCA public EPD repositories (tens of thousands of mixes)
limitations: U.S.-centric; declared values vary by PCR/plant
relevance_to_chapter: Carbon-benchmarking and EPD percentile data source
